forecast.name: Overnight
forecast.detailedForecast: Mostly cloudy, with a low around 52. South southwest wind around 9 mph.

forecast.name: Monday
forecast.detailedForecast: Mostly sunny. High near 67, with temperatures falling to around 63 in the afternoon. Southwest wind 2 to 21 mph, with gusts as high as 25 mph.

weather_report:
A gentle calm envelops Downtown San Francisco this early morning, marked by a cloak of clouds that are reluctantly parting for the promise of a brighter day. With temperatures resting at a comfortable 52 degrees, the air carries a whisper of the south-southwest breeze, a mere hint of the warmth that will follow. As forecasted, the sun will soon surrender its shyness, teasing the horizon and coaxing the city into a radiant embrace. Expect a high near 67, before the afternoon unfurls, bringing a slight dip to around 63, as the southwest winds pick up, rustling leaves and sending a refreshing splash of coolness across the streets.

As the morning unfolds, those venturing out might notice the remnants of a marine layer lingering on the outskirts, tracing the shoreline and casting an ethereal quality over the vistas. For those looking to enjoy the day, it’s wise to dress in layers—a light sweater or hoodie to ward off the early chill, yielding to a t-shirt as the sun takes its rightful place in the sky. Perhaps a leisurely walk along the waterfront as the sunlight dances on the water, or a quiet moment in one of the local parks, where flowers sway gently under the spring rays, would be a perfect way to greet this serene day.
